questions: Does the implementation correctly handle all 10 acceptance criteria from the ticket?
Are there correctness issues or edge-case bugs in the resolveTicket() control flow?
Does the implementation introduce any regressions to existing behavior?

answers.answer: Yes. All 10 acceptance criteria are satisfied. Archived tickets are resolvable by internal ID (AC#1), short ID (AC#2), and numeric number (AC#3). The shared resolver covers all 9 commands including comments (AC#4) and artifacts (AC#5). Missing refs produce not-found errors (AC#6). Existing tests pass unchanged (AC#7). Regression tests for archived-only resolution (AC#8) and cross-set numeric ambiguity (AC#9) are added. tickets list is unaffected (AC#10).
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:86-167 — resolveTicket() searches both active and archived tickets with correct priority and ambiguity handling
src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts:123-281 — 9 test cases covering all ACs pass
npm test output — 51 tests pass, 0 fail (42 existing + 9 new)
src/tickets/list.ts confirmed unmodified — does not use resolveTicket()

answers.answer: No correctness issues found. Eight control flow paths were traced through the implementation: (1) active exact ID/shortID match returns immediately, (2) active numeric match falls through for cross-set check, (3) no active match with archived exact match returns archived, (4) no active match with archived numeric match falls through to combined check, (5) cross-set ambiguity detected correctly, (6) single numeric match across combined set returned, (7) no match in either set produces not-found, (8) fetch errors produce distinct resolution-stage errors. The deviation from the plan (only returning immediately for exact matches, not numeric) is correct and necessary for AC#9.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:108-111 — Exact match early return only for ID/shortID, not numeric
src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:138-160 — Combined numeric check across both sets
src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:123-128 — Archived fetch error wrapped in distinct error message
src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts:211-226 — Cross-set ambiguity test verifies numeric refs check both sets

answers.answer: No regressions. The optional third parameter is backward-compatible (all 9 callers pass only 2 args). matchTicket() is unchanged. The active-fetch code path (lines 94-103) is identical to the original. Boundary files (list.ts, latest.ts) are unmodified. All 42 pre-existing tests pass unchanged. The only behavioral change is that archived tickets are now also searched when no active match is found — this is the intended fix.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:86-89 — Optional third parameter with default
src/tickets/index.ts — 7 call sites pass exactly 2 args, unchanged
src/comments/index.ts — 2 call sites pass exactly 2 args, unchanged
npm test output — All 42 pre-existing tests pass

statement: Code review complete. The implementation in src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts correctly implements archived ticket resolution with active-first priority, cross-set numeric ambiguity detection, and honest error reporting. All 10 acceptance criteria are satisfied. 9 new tests cover the full resolver behavior. Quality gates pass (typecheck, build, 51/51 tests). No code changes were needed by code review. The implementation is ready for verification.
